inputFields.description: You probably don't need to worry about this parameter if you're just getting the embeddings for a handful of queries. This parameter sets the maximumum number of kibiTokens (1 kibiToken = 1024 tokens) to try to stuff into a batch (to avoid out of memory errors but maximize throughput). If the total number of tokens across the flattened list of requested embeddings exceed this value, the list will be split internally and run across multiple forward passes. This will not affect the shape of your output, just the time it takes to run.

inputFields.description: normalizes returned embedding vectors to a magnitude of 1. (default: true, as this model presumes cosine similarity comparisons downstream)

inputFields.name: precision
inputFields.description: numerical precision for inference computations. Either full or half. Defaults to a paranoid value of full. You may want to test if 'half' is sufficient for your needs, though regardless you should probably prefer to use the same precision for querying as you do for archiving.

inputFields.name: query_texts
inputFields.description: A serialized JSON array of strings you wish to generate *retreival* embeddings for. (note, that you should keep this list short to avoid Replicate response size limitations). Use this to embed short text queries intended for comparison against document text. A vector will be returned corresponding to each line of text in the input array (in order of input). This endpoint will automatically format your query strings for retrieval, you do not need to preprocess them.

docstring: ✨DiffBIR: Towards Blind Image Restoration with Generative Diffusion Prior

inputFields.name: background_upsampler
inputFields.description: For 'faces' mode: Model used to upscale the background in images where the primary subject is a face.

inputFields.name: background_upsampler_tile
inputFields.description: For 'faces' mode: Size of each tile used by the background upsampler when dividing the image into patches.

inputFields.name: background_upsampler_tile_stride
inputFields.description: For 'faces' mode: Distance between the start of each tile when the background is divided for upscaling. A smaller stride means more overlap between tiles.

inputFields.name: color_fix_type
inputFields.description: Method used for color correction post enhancement. 'wavelet' and 'adain' offer different styles of color correction, while 'none' skips this step.

inputFields.name: disable_preprocess_model
inputFields.description: Disables the initial preprocessing step using SwinIR. Turn this off if your input image is already of high quality and doesn't require restoration.

inputFields.name: face_detection_model
inputFields.description: For 'faces' mode: Model used for detecting faces in the image. Choose based on accuracy and speed preferences.

inputFields.name: guidance_repeat
inputFields.description: For 'general_scenes': Number of times the guidance process is repeated during enhancement.

inputFields.name: guidance_scale
inputFields.description: For 'general_scenes': Scale factor for the guidance mechanism. Adjusts the influence of guidance on the enhancement process.

inputFields.name: guidance_space
inputFields.description: For 'general_scenes': Determines in which space (RGB or latent) the guidance operates. 'latent' can often provide more subtle and context-aware enhancements.

inputFields.name: guidance_time_start
inputFields.description: For 'general_scenes': Specifies when (at which step) the guidance mechanism starts influencing the enhancement.

inputFields.name: guidance_time_stop
inputFields.description: For 'general_scenes': Specifies when (at which step) the guidance mechanism stops influencing the enhancement.

inputFields.name: has_aligned
inputFields.description: For 'faces' mode: Indicates if the input images are already cropped and aligned to faces. If not, the model will attempt to do this.

inputFields.name: input
inputFields.description: Path to the input image you want to enhance.

inputFields.name: only_center_face
inputFields.description: For 'faces' mode: If multiple faces are detected, only enhance the center-most face in the image.

inputFields.name: reload_restoration_model
inputFields.description: Reload the image restoration model (SwinIR) if set to True. This can be useful if you've updated or changed the underlying SwinIR model.

inputFields.name: repeat_times
inputFields.description: Number of times the enhancement process is repeated by feeding the output back as input. This can refine the result but might also introduce over-enhancement issues.

inputFields.name: restoration_model_type
inputFields.description: Select the restoration model that aligns with the content of your image. This model is responsible for image restoration which removes degradations.

inputFields.name: seed
inputFields.description: Random seed to ensure reproducibility. Setting this ensures that multiple runs with the same input produce the same output.

inputFields.name: steps
inputFields.description: The number of enhancement iterations to perform. More steps might result in a clearer image but can also introduce artifacts.

inputFields.name: super_resolution_factor
inputFields.description: Factor by which the input image resolution should be increased. For instance, a factor of 4 will make the resolution 4 times greater in both height and width.

inputFields.name: tile_size
inputFields.description: Size of each tile (or patch) when 'tiled' option is enabled. Determines how the image is divided during patch-based enhancement.

inputFields.name: tile_stride
inputFields.description: Distance between the start of each tile when the image is divided for patch-based enhancement. A smaller stride means more overlap between tiles.

inputFields.name: tiled
inputFields.description: Whether to use patch-based sampling. This can be useful for very large images to enhance them in smaller chunks rather than all at once.

inputFields.name: upscaling_model_type
inputFields.description: Choose the type of model best suited for the primary content of the image: 'faces' for portraits and 'general_scenes' for everything else.

inputFields.name: use_guidance
inputFields.description: Use latent image guidance for enhancement. This can help in achieving more accurate and contextually relevant enhancements.

inputFields.name: length_penalty
inputFields.description: A parameter that controls how long the outputs are. If < 1, the model will tend to generate shorter outputs, and > 1 will tend to generate longer outputs.

inputFields.name: max_new_tokens
inputFields.description: This parameter has been renamed to max_tokens. max_new_tokens only exists for backwards compatibility purposes. We recommend you use max_tokens instead. Both may not be specified.

inputFields.name: max_tokens
inputFields.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate. A word is generally 2-3 tokens.

inputFields.name: min_new_tokens
inputFields.description: This parameter has been renamed to min_tokens. min_new_tokens only exists for backwards compatibility purposes. We recommend you use min_tokens instead. Both may not be specified.

inputFields.name: min_tokens
inputFields.description: Minimum number of tokens to generate. To disable, set to -1. A word is generally 2-3 tokens.

inputFields.name: presence_penalty
inputFields.description: A parameter that penalizes repeated tokens regardless of the number of appearances. As the value increases, the model will be less likely to repeat tokens in the output.

inputFields.name: prompt
inputFields.description: Prompt to send to the model.

inputFields.name: prompt_template
inputFields.description: Template for formatting the prompt. Can be an arbitrary string, but must contain the substring '{prompt}'.

inputFields.name: seed
inputFields.description: Random seed. Leave blank to randomize the seed.

inputFields.name: stop_sequences
inputFields.description: A comma-separated list of sequences to stop generation at. For example, '<end>,<stop>' will stop generation at the first instance of 'end' or '<stop>'.

inputFields.name: temperature
inputFields.description: Adjusts randomness of outputs, greater than 1 is random and 0 is deterministic, 0.75 is a good starting value.

inputFields.name: top_k
inputFields.description: When decoding text, samples from the top k most likely tokens; lower to ignore less likely tokens.

inputFields.name: top_p
inputFields.description: When decoding text, samples from the top p percentage of most likely tokens; lower to ignore less likely tokens.

inputFields.name: instructions
inputFields.description: A system (or developer) message inserted into the model's context. When using along with previous_response_id, the instructions from a previous response will not be carried over to the next response. This makes it simple to swap out system (or developer) messages in new responses.

inputFields.name: json_schema
inputFields.description: A JSON schema that the response must conform to. For simple data structures we recommend using 'simple_text_format_schema' which will be converted to a JSON schema for you.

inputFields.name: max_output_tokens
inputFields.description: Maximum number of completion tokens to generate. For higher reasoning efforts you may need to increase your max_completion_tokens to avoid empty responses (where all the tokens are used on reasoning).

inputFields.name: model
inputFields.description: GPT-5 model to use.

inputFields.name: previous_response_id
inputFields.description: The ID of a previous response to continue from.

inputFields.name: prompt
inputFields.description: A simple text input to the model, equivalent to a text input with the user role. Ignored if input_item_list is provided.

inputFields.name: reasoning_effort
inputFields.description: Constrains effort on reasoning for GPT-5 models. Currently supported values are minimal, low, medium, and high. The minimal value gets answers back faster without extensive reasoning first. Reducing reasoning effort can result in faster responses and fewer tokens used on reasoning in a response. For higher reasoning efforts you may need to increase your max_completion_tokens to avoid empty responses (where all the tokens are used on reasoning).

inputFields.name: simple_schema
inputFields.description: Create a JSON schema for the output to conform to. The schema will be created from a simple list of field specifications. Strings: 'thing' (defaults to string), 'thing:str', 'thing:string'. Booleans: 'is_a_thing:bool' or 'is_a_thing:boolean'. Numbers: 'count:number', 'count:int'. Lists: 'things:list' (defaults to list of strings), 'things:list:str', 'number_things:list:number', etc. Nested objects are not supported, use 'json_schema' instead.

inputFields.name: tools
inputFields.description: Tools to make available to the model. Should be a JSON object containing a list of tool definitions.

inputFields.name: verbosity
inputFields.description: Constrains the verbosity of the model's response. Lower values will result in more concise responses, while higher values will result in more verbose responses. Currently supported values are low, medium, and high. GPT-5 supports this parameter to help control whether answers are short and to the point or long and comprehensive.
